title: Verb ようか Verbるまいか
titleEn: Expresses indecision: Should I do it or not?
structure: Verb-volitional form + か + Verb-volitional negative form (まい) + か
structureEn: Verb-volitional form + か + Verb-volitional negative form (まい) + か
explanation: Dùng để diễn tả sự phân vân có nên làm hay không làm một việc. Mẫu này trang trọng hơn cách nói しようかどうか. Cấu trúc: Verb-volitional form + か + Verb-volitional negative form (まい) + か.
explanationEn: This grammar shows hesitation about whether to do something or not. The volitional form plus まい (negative volitional) suggests an internal debate about two opposite choices of the *same* verb
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: Verb ようが Verb るまいが
titleEn: Expresses whether or not, no matter if something happens or not
structure: Verb-volitional form + が + Verb-negative volitional form + が
structureEn: Verb-volitional form + が + Verb-negative volitional form + が
explanation: Dùng để nói dù có làm hay không làm X thì kết quả hoặc thái độ vẫn không thay đổi. Cấu trúc: Verb-volitional form + が + Verb-negative volitional form + が.
explanationEn: Verbようが Verbるまいが indicates that whatever happens—whether the action is done or not—the outcome remains unchanged or the speakers stance remains the same
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: Verb ようがない
titleEn: Expresses no way to do something, impossible to do
explanation: Dùng để nói không có cách nào để làm việc đó, vì thiếu phương tiện, điều kiện hoặc khả năng. Cấu trúc: Verb (masu stem) + ようがない.
explanationEn: 〜ようがない is used to say that theres no way or method to do something, even if you wanted to
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: Verb ようと Verbる まいと
titleEn: This structure indicates that regardless of whether one does (verb) or not, the outcome/attitude remains unchanged
structure: Verb-volitional + と + Verb-negative volitional (～まい) + と
structureEn: Verb-volitional + と + Verb-negative volitional (～まい) + と
explanation: Dùng để nói bất kể có làm hay không làm X, điều phía sau vẫn không đổi. Sắc thái trang trọng, văn viết. Cấu trúc: Verb-volitional + と + Verb-negative volitional (～まい) + と.
explanationEn: The expression Verb-volitional form + と + Verb-negative volitional (～まい) + と means regardless of whether one does X or not, it doesnt change the situation. It shows the speakers firm stance or the unchanging nature of some outcome
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: Verb ようにも
titleEn: Used to express even though one wants to do something, one cant for some reason
structure: Verb-volitional form + にも + (reason/potential negative, etc.)
structureEn: Verb-volitional form + にも + (reason/potential negative, etc.)
explanation: Dùng khi muốn làm X nhưng không thể vì có lý do cản trở. Thường đi với vế sau nêu lý do hoặc dạng phủ định khả năng. Cấu trúc: Verb-volitional form + にも + (reason/potential negative, etc.).
explanationEn: The ～ようにも grammar point indicates that a person wants or tries to do something (volitional form), but for some reason cannot. Often, theres a second clause explaining what prevents the action
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: Verb ようにも Verb れない
titleEn: Expresses the inability to do something even if one wants/tries
structure: Verb-volitional form + にも + (potential negative form / reason)
structureEn: Verb-volitional form + にも + (potential negative form / reason)
explanation: Dùng để nói dù muốn hoặc cố làm X cũng không thể làm được. Mẫu này nhấn mạnh sự bất lực do hoàn cảnh. Cấu trúc: Verb-volitional form + にも + (potential negative form / reason).
explanationEn: This grammar shows that you want (or try) to do something (volitional form), but cannot due to some obstacle. Often it appears as Verb-volitional + にも + Verb (potential negative) or simply Verb-volitional + にも + (reason you cant)
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: Verb ようもない
titleEn: Expresses its impossible to do something
structure: Verb-ます stem + ようもない
structureEn: Verb-ます stem + ようもない
explanation: Dùng để nói hoàn toàn không có cách nào để làm X. Sắc thái tương tự ようがない nhưng thường nhấn mạnh mạnh hơn. Cấu trúc: Verb-ます stem + ようもない.
explanationEn: ～ようもない typically attaches to the verbs ます-stem, adding ようもない to indicate that there is no way or no method to perform the action. It’s the same nuance as Verbようがない in many references
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: Verbる がままに
titleEn: Expresses letting something happen as it is; just as something does
explanation: Dùng để nói để một sự việc diễn ra tự nhiên theo đúng trạng thái hoặc dòng chảy của nó. Có thể hiểu là “cứ theo như”. Cấu trúc: Verb-dictionary form + がままに.
explanationEn: がままに describes an action happening exactly as is, without intervention or change, often suggesting doing something passively or naturally
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: Verbる が早いか
titleEn: Expresses as soon as... or the moment when
explanation: Dùng để nói ngay khi hành động X xảy ra thì hành động Y xảy ra gần như lập tức. Mẫu này trang trọng và nhấn mạnh tốc độ. Cấu trúc: Verb-dictionary form + が早いか.
explanationEn: Verbる が早いか indicates that something happens immediately after something else. Its similar to as soon as... or no sooner than
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)
